codeReview.summary: Pass. Compared against the failed review for m4-fix-backfill-storage-precision-monotonic-compare, this fix now provides explicit runtime-path proof through UsageAggregator.refreshAll() for both success and persistence-failure guard semantics. The updated tests assert success-path cursor/version advancement and failure-path persistence guard activation with unchanged cursor state, resolving the prior blocking findings.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Procedure adherence reporting appears overstated for token-accounting-worker context-read requirements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: token-accounting-worker/SKILL.md requires reading mission.md, AGENTS.md, .factory/library/architecture.md, and .factory/library/user-testing.md before marking followedProcedure=true. The reviewed transcript skeleton for workerSessionId fb8d5177-8221-4a48-b65d-c54cd8d8a81b shows mission.md/validation-contract.md/features.json reads and code/test actions, while handoff verification.commandsRun records only mission.md/read + refreshAll grep evidence; no concrete AGENTS.md or library architecture/user-testing read evidence is recorded.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared library docs remain misaligned with this mission's token-accounting/backfill scope.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md and .factory/library/user-testing.md are still switcher-focused (profile switching, browser/CLI launch, cross-surface switcher UI flows), which does not reflect the m4 reconciliation/backfill/token-accounting domain reviewed here.

summary: Pass: this fix adequately resolves the prior blocking runtime-coverage findings by validating UsageAggregator.refreshAll() guard behavior in both success and persistence-failure paths. No new blocking correctness or security issues were found.
